title: Widen GET /api/agents to {agents, shells}
action: Widen GET /api/agents's JSON response from a bare Vec<AgentRow> to one object {"agents": AgentRow[], "shells": ShellRow[]} (per plan.md's Approach -- one round trip, no new endpoint). Add ShellRow { pane_id, workspace_id, workspace_label, tab_label, path: Option<String> } to src/web/api.rs. shells is built by: (1) collecting the set of workspace_ids that have at least one agent from snap.agents, (2) iterating snap.panes and keeping only panes whose workspace_id is NOT in that set (per D3 -- zero-agent workspaces only, never a shell pane inside a workspace that already has agents), (3) for each kept pane, path = pane.foreground_cwd.clone().or(pane.cwd.clone()) -- read directly off that specific pane, NOT through Snapshot::anchor_for_workspace (that join answers a different question: which pane seeds something NEW created in a workspace; here the pane itself is already known, per plan.md's Discovery note). workspace_label and tab_label need a workspace_id/tab_id-keyed lookup -- either widen workspace_label_for/tab_label_for to accept an id directly (updating their existing &Agent call sites to pass agent.workspace_id/agent.tab_id) or add sibling id-keyed helpers; either is fine, prefer whichever keeps the existing Agent-based call sites byte-identical in behavior. Existing AgentRow output and its field values must not change at all. FakeHerdr's w3 seed currently has exactly one shell pane -- extend it (or add a sibling workspace) with a SECOND shell pane so a workspace with 2+ shells is provable (D1's per-pane granularity must produce multiple rows, not one); do NOT touch the existing w3 workspace's agent_status value (that's a separate, already-filed friction item, out of scope here). tests/observe_reply_e2e.rs's observe_then_reply_round_trips test currently parses GET /api/agents as a bare Vec<serde_json::Value> and asserts agents.len() == 4 (line ~46-55) -- update it to parse the new {agents, shells} object and assert on the agents field specifically (still 4), since this response shape changes structurally. Name every new unit test with the prefix homeshell_, and write at least 3 of them (the cell's verify command greps for at least 3 passing tests matching that exact prefix).

truths: GET /api/agents returns a JSON object with 'agents' and 'shells' keys, never a bare array
shells contains one entry per pane belonging to a workspace with zero agents; a workspace with at least one agent contributes zero shell entries even if it also has plain-shell panes
A workspace with 2+ shell panes and zero agents produces 2+ separate shell entries, one per pane
Each shell entry's path is that specific pane's own foreground_cwd (preferred) or cwd, never a value resolved via anchor_for_workspace
The existing agents array's values are byte-identical to what GET /api/agents returned before this change
tests/observe_reply_e2e.rs's observe_then_reply_round_trips test parses the new {agents, shells} response shape and still asserts the agents field has length 4
Every pre-existing herdr and web test stays green, with agents_lists_flat_snapshot updated to parse the new {agents, shells} shape rather than a bare array

artifacts.substantive: ShellRow struct and the widened agents() handler, no stubs

key_links: agents() constructs both agents and shells from the same snapshot fetch, still one round trip
prohibitions: Do not change any existing AgentRow field's meaning or value
Do not use anchor_for_workspace for a shell entry's path -- read the specific pane's own cwd/foreground_cwd directly
Do not touch src/web/create.rs or src/web/auth.rs
Do not change w3's existing agent_status value in fake.rs -- that is separately filed friction, out of scope here

verify: cargo test --quiet && cargo fmt --all --check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test --lib -- homeshell_ 2>&1 | grep -qE 'test result: ok\. ([3-9]|[1-9][0-9]+) passed'

trace.outcome: Widened GET /api/agents to {agents, shells}: added ShellRow/AgentsResponse to src/web/api.rs, id-keyed workspace_label_for_id/tab_label_for_id to src/herdr/wire.rs, a second w3 shell pane to src/herdr/fake.rs's seed, and updated observe_reply_e2e.rs to parse the new shape. AgentRow output unchanged.

checks.id: GET /api/agents returns a JSON object with 'agents' and 'shells' keys, never a bare array
checks.evidence: src/web/api.rs:41-48 AgentsResponse{agents,shells} struct; handler returns Json(AgentsResponse{agents: rows, shells}) replacing old Json(rows) bare array

checks.id: shells contains one entry per pane in a zero-agent workspace; a workspace with any agent contributes zero shell entries
checks.evidence: agent_workspaces = HashSet of snap.agents workspace_ids; filter keeps panes where !agent_workspaces.contains(p.workspace_id). w2 has agents so w2:p5 excluded; test homeshell_workspace_with_agents_contributes_no_shell_rows confirms

checks.id: A workspace with 2+ shell panes and zero agents produces 2+ separate shell entries
checks.evidence: fake.rs adds pane w3:p7 alongside w3:p6; test homeshell_multi_shell_workspace_produces_one_row_per_pane asserts len==2

checks.id: Each shell entry's path is read off that pane's own foreground_cwd/cwd, not anchor_for_workspace
checks.evidence: path: p.foreground_cwd.clone().or(p.cwd.clone()); no anchor_for_workspace call in handler; test confirms distinct per-pane paths

checks.id: Existing agents array values byte-identical to before
checks.evidence: AgentRow map block untouched, all 9 fields identical; only change is the response wrapper
